Synapse survived the drop, but in-flight requests got wedged and never timed out. We should have application level timeouts for outbound HTTP requests.

Failure to refresh upon switching project  Quite a few times now I've found myself staring at an out-of-date STATUS or BRANCHES tab, being thoroughly confused about what's going on, e.g. why certain files don't exist, or why the STATUS screen shows nothing modified even though I know for sure there are some modified files. The confusion usually lasts a couple of seconds before I realize I need to close the tab and re-open it to refresh it.  ## How to reproduce  Make sure you have two projects, let's call them A and B. Project A has one directory tracked by git.   - Open Project A (Ctrl + Alt + P) - Make sure it's on master and has no modified files - Open a STATUS and a BRANCHES view in their own tabs - Switch to project B using Ctrl + Alt + P - Switch to a different branch in project A through your terminal - Edit some file from project A through some other means (`nano`, `vim`, `echo/cat`, whatever floats your boat) - Switch back to project A using Ctrl + Alt + P - Obser

`traces_sampler` receives always "generic ASGI request" as transaction name.  ### How do you use Sentry?  Sentry Saas (sentry.io)  ### Version  1.11.1  ### Steps to Reproduce  I have manually instrumented traces_sampler counted on transaction name in sampling context. At some point with sentry-sdk version upgrade, it stopped work - there is always "generic ASGI request" in transaction name. But at the performance page, transaction has a proper name.  How we initiate sentry FastAPI integration: ``` sentry_sdk.init(     dsn=SENTRY_DSN,     environment="dev",     traces_sampler=sentry_traces_sampler,     integrations=[         starlette.StarletteIntegration(transaction_style="endpoint"),         fastapi.FastApiIntegration(transaction_style="endpoint"),`     ] ) ```  The traces_sampler function: ``` def sentry_traces_sampler(sampling_context):     transaction_sampling_mapper = {         "app.api.api_v1.routers.users.some_name": 0.1,         "app.api.api_v1.routers.messa

Files incorrectly flagged as merge conflicts during inline diff  For some files, when I try to start an inline diff it is flagged as containing a merge conflict and the diff does not open.  I tracked down the source of this and it was introduced in https://github.com/timbrel/GitSavvy/commit/8df9b46a4d34014949286aaecad3a6398fa1ee7b, specifically in `safely_parse_metadata`  The issue seems to be that the regex is picking up extra results based on the content of the diff, in addition to the metadata. As an example, for the input:  ``` @@ -7,0 +8 @@ RCall = "6f49c342-dc21-5d91-9882-a32aef131414" ```  the output is `[(7, 0), (8, 1), (5, 1), (9882, 1)]`  Another example is   ``` @@ -295 +313 @@ docs-main:iai-2.0.0: ```  giving `[(295, 1), (313, 1), (2, 1)]`  It seems like the previous version was limiting the search to inside the `@@ ... @@` whereas now it is not, and it is flagging the diff a merge conflict because there are more than two elements in the returned metadat

Impossible to combine JoinDocuments and JoinAnswers  Creating a pipeline using JoinDocuments and JoinAnswer, leads to the following error: ``` File "/home/ubuntu/haystack/haystack/nodes/other/join_answers.py", line 45, in <listcomp>     reader_results = [inp["answers"] for inp in inputs] KeyError: 'answers' ```  The reason for this is that [these lines](https://github.com/deepset-ai/haystack/blob/b6986ea25d7bbd06158dcb9b2f2b9187266accff/haystack/nodes/base.py#L237-L239) cause the `inputs` parameter of the first join node to be propagated down the pipeline. This eventually leads to [this condition](https://github.com/deepset-ai/haystack/blob/b6986ea25d7bbd06158dcb9b2f2b9187266accff/haystack/pipelines/base.py#L530) being false when constructing the input for the next join node resulting in that error.

Bug: branch interface doesn't open graph for selected branch  When in branch interface, hit `[g]` to open graph under for the branch under the cursor. Instead, the graph view opens with the checked out branch, not the branch under cursor.  - Sublime Text 3 3184 - Git 2.19.1 - OS type and version 10.14.1 (18B75) - Console error output -nothing  Git bisect points at 6a6c612 as the culprit, and we can see the `LogGraphMixin` command no longer uses `get_graph_args`, and users of the mixin need to use `prepare_target_view` to set additional graph arguments as view settings.

ui.refreshable updating the wrong element  ### Description  This observation originates from a StackOverflow question: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/76763206/is-it-possible-to-use-widgets-in-nicegui-python  The following code creates two refreshable buttons, each of which refreshes itself and notifies the user about it. But independent of which button is clicked, it always refreshes button B.  ```py class Test:      def __init__(self, name):         self.name = name         self.ui()      @ui.refreshable     def ui(self):         ui.notify(f'Refreshing {self.name}')         ui.button(self.name, on_click=self.ui.refresh)  Test('A') Test('B') ```  The problem can be avoided by replacing ```py on_click=self.ui.refresh ``` with ```py on_click=lambda: self.ui.refresh() ```  But what is the reason for this mixup, and how to fix it?

Dashboards don't work well with emoji in branch names  For example, `this-branch-is-da-💥` works fine from the command line, but the Status dashboard shows `None.` as the branch name, the branch dashboard doesn't show the branch at all, and the rebase dashboard doesn't function correctly.  This is likely due to an omission in a regular expression we're using for branch name detection somewhere.

In the tags dashboard the commit reading is naive  For the text commands we read the whole line and then just `line[4:11]` to get the commit hash.  This **is** naive as the hash length is not a fixed value.  🙄   Some commands might benefit from reading out the tag name instead as well.

The invocation of dispatcher.storage.close() performs these 2 operations.  There is no bug in restoring data: if saving was successful, everything works correctly.   ### Steps to Reproduce  1. Run [this](https://gist.github.com/Birdi7/ffb3077b89d81f897df0d51072a8dbbd) gist. The JSONStorage is used there, but

Open file from diff in word-diff mode throws  This is a regression of the accurate jumping. Honestly I hardly knew about the word diff mode, and it's not part of my workflow but the accurate jumping expects a unified diff otherwise it will raise bc it depends on the line mode char +- on each line.  There is no trivial fix I guess bc for accurate jumping we need to differentiate between available and removed chars.  Probably three options.  1. As a hotfix just disable the open functionality. 2. Do inaccurate jumping as before. 3. Just parse it 🤪 as well.

use_syntax_for_commit_editmsg setting to use native syntax doesn't work in some cases  `use_syntax_for_commit_editmsg` setting, to use sublime's built-in syntax for commit messages, doesn't work in case when commit file is opened from external editor (from command line for example using  `git commit`).  I see in the code that there is GitCommit command that switches to native syntax but that command is obviously not trigged in that case (because commit is triggered externally). There is also a load listener which still applies gitsavvy commit syntax, without considering `use_syntax_for_commit_editmsg` setting. That seems to be the bug.  I expect that when `use_syntax_for_commit_editmsg` is enabled, native syntax will be used for git commit files, regardless of how commit file is opened.  Sublime 3 b3161 git version 2.15.1 (Apple Git-101) macOs 10.13

optimize_for_target_gateset OOMs on large CircuitOps  **Description of the issue**  When a circuit containing a large (in this case, 17 qubits & depth 20) CircuitOp is passed to `optimize_for_target_gateset`, it generates an OOM, possibly crashing the device. This circuit is well within the realm of "easy to simulate", so we shouldn't be OOMing or should at least warn users that this has a lower threshold than simulation.  **How to reproduce the issue**  ``` qnum = 17 qubits = cirq.LineQubit.range(qnum) subcircuit = cirq.FrozenCircuit(     *[       cirq.CX(qubits[i], qubits[i+1])       for i in range(0, qnum-1, 2)     ],     *[       cirq.CZ(qubits[i], qubits[i+1])       for i in range(1, qnum-1, 2)     ], ) circuit = cirq.Circuit(     cirq.CircuitOperation(subcircuit).repeat(10),     cirq.measure(*qubits, key='out') ) print(circuit) # OOM: cannot allocate 256GB supported_circuit = cirq.optimize_for_target_gateset(     circuit,     context=cirq.TransformerCon
